channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: US strikes on a Yemeni oil port kill 74 people, Houthis say, in deadliest attack of Trump campaign
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — U.S. airstrikes targeting an oil port held by Yemen’s Houthi rebels killed 74 people and wounded 171 others, the group said Friday, marking the single-deadliest known attack under President Donald Trump’s new campaign targeting the rebels.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli strikes kill at least 17 in Gaza and Huckabee makes first appearance as US ambassador
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli airstrikes across Gaza killed at least 17 people early Friday including children, hospital workers said, as the new U.S. ambassador to Israel made his first public appearance in Jerusalem.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rubio says the US will drop Ukraine-Russia peace efforts if no progress within days
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Friday that the U.S. may “move on” from trying to secure a Russia-Ukraine peace deal if there is no progress in the coming days, after months of efforts have failed to bring an end to the fighting. He spoke in Paris after landmark talks among U.S.
item.dc:creator: ANGELA CHARLTON and HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: China’s leader ends Southeast Asia tour that touted Beijing's reliability vs. US tariff threats
item.description: PHNOM PENH, Cambodia (AP) — Chinese President Xi Jinping on Friday completed a two-day state visit to Cambodia, capping a three-nation Southeast Asia tour promoting Beijing's reliability as the region faces economic uncertainty due to U.S. President Donald Trump’s tariff proposals.
item.dc:creator: SOPHENG CHEANG and GRANT PECK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran-US talks over Tehran's nuclear program hinge on a billionaire and a seasoned diplomat
item.description: As far as biographies go, the two men in charge of the delicate negotiations between Iran and the United States over Tehran’s rapidly advancing nuclear program couldn’t be more different.
item.dc:creator: Jon Gambrell And Amir Vahdat, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US man hijacks small plane in Belize, stabs 3 people before he's shot dead
item.description: Authorities at the Philip S.W. Goldson International Airport reported a hijacking of a small plan by a U.S. man who stabbed two passengers and a pilot before being fatally shot.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US lawmakers' bipartisan Taiwan visit signals support despite harsh words and tariffs from Trump
item.description: Republican and Democratic lawmakers are making their first trip to Taiwan under the new Trump administration a bipartisan one.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Bodeen, Ellen Knickmeyer And Simina Mistreanu, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Carney says lower internal trade barriers will help Canada more than Trump's tariffs will harm it
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Prime Minister Mark Carney said Thursday eliminating trade barriers within Canada would benefit Canadians far more than U.S. President Donald Trump can ever take away with his trade war as he made his case to retain power at the last debate ahead of the April 28 vote.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia strikes Ukrainian city of Dnipro as Kyiv seeks security pledges in Paris
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian forces launched a massive drone attack on the southeastern Ukrainian city of Dnipro, killing three people, including a child, and wounding dozens, officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A recovering Pope Francis visits Rome prison to keep annual Holy Week appointment
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — Pope Francis visited Rome's central prison Thursday and met with dozens of inmates as he kept an Easter season appointment to spend Holy Thursday among the least fortunate, even as he continues recovering from a life-threatening bout of pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Thousands of Gaza children are malnourished under Israel's food blockade, aid groups say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Aid groups are raising new alarm over Israel’s blockade of the war-ravaged Gaza Strip, where it has barred entry of all food and other goods for more than six weeks.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, FATMA KHALED and LEE KEATH, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Haitians face record hunger as gang violence grips country in throes of economic crisis
item.description: PORT-AU-PRINCE, Haiti (AP) — More than half of Haiti’s population is expected to experience severe hunger through June, and another 8,400 people living in makeshift shelters are projected to starve, according to a new report released this week.
item.dc:creator: EVENS SANON and DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Van Hollen blocked from visiting mistakenly deported man in El Salvador
item.description: Sen. Chris Van Hollen (D-Md.) said he and a lawyer for the family of Kilmar Abrego Garcia were stopped at a security checkpoint in El Salvador on Thursday when they tried to visit the mistakenly deported man at CECOT prison.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, The Hill

item.title: Trump says US-Ukraine minerals deal could be signed this month
item.description: President Trump said Thursday he expects to sign a minerals agreement with Ukraine in the coming days, a few weeks after an expected deal signing was called off following a contentious Oval Office meeting with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, The Hill

item.title: Trump takes center stage in Canada’s prime minister election debate
item.description: Canada's electoral debate for the next prime minister focused on who is best equipped to counter President Trump amid his tariff war and his 51st state comments.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: ICC opens inquiry into Hungary for failing to arrest Netanyahu
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — Judges at the International Criminal Court want Hungary to explain why it failed to arrest Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu when he visited Budapest earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former FAA contractor pleads guilty to sharing US airport files with Iran
item.description: A former Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) contractor pleaded guilty Wednesday to working as an agent of Iran and sharing sensitive U.S. airport data with Iranian intelligence officers. Abouzar Rahmati, 42, admitted to conspiring to act and acting as an agent of Iran on U.S.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Former MLS player Aaron Boupendza dies after falling from building, soccer officials say
item.description: The Gabonese soccer federation announced Wednesday that Aaron Boupendza, a striker for its men's national team, has died after falling from the 11th floor of a building in China.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump touts ‘productive’ chats with Mexico, Japan amid tariff battle
item.description: President Trump shared that he had “productive” discussions with Mexico and Japan as countries look to strike deals with the U.S. over the president’s trade agenda. “Had a very productive call with the President of Mexico yesterday,” Trump said Thursday in a Truth Social post.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Israeli troops will remain in Gaza buffer zones indefinitely, Defense Minister Katz says
item.description: Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz says troops will indefinitely remain in the buffer zone they created in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Italian prime minister meets with Trump on tariffs
item.description: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni is expected to speak for Italy and the European Union as a whole.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Portrait of Palestinian boy who lost both arms is World Press Photo of the year for 2025
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — A portrait of a young Palestinian boy who lost both arms as a result of an Israeli attack in Gaza was honored Thursday as World Press Photo of the year.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CORDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A Berlin doctor has been charged with the killings of 15 patients under palliative care
item.description: A German doctor has been charged with murdering over 15 people under palliative care; he has also been accused of attempting to cover up the crimes by starting fires in their homes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Van Hollen denied meeting, phone call with Abrego Garcia during El Salvador visit
item.description: The Salvadoran government on Wednesday rebuffed Sen. Chris Van Hollen’s (D-Md.) request to meet or speak with a wrongfully deported Maryland man, the senator said, accusing the Trump administration and others of lying about Kilmar Abrego Garcia’s gang ties.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: UN envoy urges Security Council to try to prevent renewed civil war in South Sudan
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The top United Nations official in South Sudan urged the U.N. Security Council on Wednesday to use its clout to prevent the world’s newest nation from again plunging into civil war.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK's top court says definition of a woman is based on biological sex and excludes transgender people
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The U.K. Supreme Court ruled Wednesday that a woman is someone born biologically female, excluding transgender people from the legal definition in a long-running dispute between a feminist group and the Scottish government.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, JILL LAWLESS and SYLVIA HUI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: UK Supreme Court issues landmark ruling on legal definition of woman
item.description: The Supreme Court of the United Kingdom unanimously ruled Wednesday that the legal definition of a woman is someone born biologically female.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia-linked hackers targeting European diplomats with invites to bogus wine tasting events
item.description: A Russia-linked hacking group is trying to get European diplomats to download malicious software through invitations to fake wine tasting events, a report says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: American pastor kidnapped in South Africa rescued after deadly police shootout
item.description: American pastor Josh Sullivan, who was kidnapped from his church in the Eastern Cape Province last week, has been rescued by police following a "high-intensity shootout.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Maryland man's family questions death, says he didn't drown in Bahamas
item.description: Dinari McAlmont of Bowie, Maryland, was found dead April 5 near the Atlantis resort on Paradise Island, according to Royal Bahamian Police.
item.dc:creator: Rich McHugh, NewsNation

item.title: Hong Kong post office will stop shipping parcels to US over tariffs
item.description: The U.S. earlier announced it would end a customs exception allowing small-value parcels from Hong Kong to enter the U.S. without tax.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strike outside a Gaza hospital kills a medic and wounds several
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli airstrike hit the northern gate of the Kuwaiti Field Hospital in the Gaza Strip on Tuesday, killing a medic and wounding nine other people — all patients and medics — according to a spokesman for the hospital in the Muwasi area, where hundreds of thousands
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Putin sees Trump as 'an easy mark': John Bolton
item.description: During Monday's meeting with the press, Trump said of Ukraine: "When you start a war, you've gotta know that you can win the war.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: New report raises questions over the origins of COVID
item.description: A military report revealed that seven U.S. service members had COVID-like symptoms during an event in Wuhan in October of 2019.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: How a penguin in a cardboard box caused a helicopter crash in South Africa
item.description: A penguin inside a carboard box led to a helicopter crash in South Africa, the country's Civil Aviation Authority said in a report this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tragic NYC helicopter crash hits home in Barcelona, where victims were well known
item.description: BARCELONA, Spain (AP) — As they sat inside a helicopter shortly before a flight over the towering skyscrapers of New York City, Agustin Escobar flashed a thumbs-up while his wife and children beamed big smiles.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH WILSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Woman unknowingly gives birth to a stranger's baby after IVF clinic error
item.description: A woman in Australia unknowingly gave birth to a stranger’s baby after she received another patient's embryo from her in vitro fertilization clinic due to “human error,” the clinic said.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
